info.title: MoIP Controller API
info.description:
# API
## Introduction
The REST API for interacting with MoIP Controllers. This API contains the MoIP specific functionallity as well as the more generic device functionallity shared with other devices.
## Authentication
This API, and any other device specific APIs, will require obtaining a JSON Web Token(JWT) using the [Digest Login](#tag/Authentication/operation/digestLogin) or [Post Login](#tag/Authentication/operation/postLogin) endpoints prior to use. JWTs are used since different parts of the API, and the different APIs, may be handled by separate services internally.
## ID Values
The component IDs are used to reference components from other components, ie. pairing, video wall membership, etc. They are also intended to be used as external references and, as such, won't change for the life of the component. If a unit is deleted and it's rediscovered then it will get new IDs but would count as a 'new' unit from the standpoint of the Controller. The IDs are also unique across the entire Controller and not just to the given component type.

## Groups
The 'group_rx's and 'group_tx's are the intended logical grouping of the audio, video, serial, IR, and USB components to make up an RX or TX. While this is the intended grouping, the individual components can be paired differently if this is desired. For example, watching video from one TX while listening to a different TX.

## Updates
While the API can be used in a purely synchronous fashion, status changes, and more importantly serial messages, can be handled without polling by using the [event websocket](#tag/Eventing/operation/websocket) to get asynchronous notifications of changes.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Path endpoints for core MoIP system items.

tags.name: Eventing
tags.description: Path endpoints, and pseudo path endpoints, for reporting change events.

tags.description: Path endpoints for all of the various MoIP endpoints available in the system.

tags.description: Path endpoints for video wall compositions.

get.description: Returns base level device information.

get.summary: Get JWT for API access using digest.
get.description: This endpoint uses HTTP Digest for authentication.

post.summary: Get JWT for API access using post.
post.description: Since the password is sent in the clear for this request, it really should only be used if HTTPS is being used.

get.description: Get the current device configuration

put.description: Upload and apply the device configuration

get.description: Returns firmware information
Returns hardware information
Returns lan information
Returns hardware statistical information
Returns time information

get.summary: Get the combined debug and error log.
get.description: Get the combined debug and error log.

delete.summary: Clear both debug and error logs.
delete.description: Clear both debug and error logs.

get.description: Get the debug log.
Get the error log.

get.summary: Download the combined debug and error log.
get.description: Download the combined debug and error log.

get.summary: Get raw change event socket information.
get.description: This describes a raw socket that sends changes. The websocket is the preferred solution but this may be used as a fallback. This socket works just like the change endpoints just in a simpler form, text over a raw socket. Any data sent on this socket will be dropped. Change events are '\r\n' deliminated lines in the form of [ADD | MOD | DEL] [path change] or PNG -. ex. MOD /api/v1/moip/unit/1022

WEBSOCKET: This is only a placeholder since OpenAPI doesn't describe websockets natively. The JSON response schema does describe the message format received on the websocket. While the websocket protocol field isn't normally used, any value can be given, it can used to supply the JWT for authorization since browsers restrict access to the request headers. Using a protocol of the form 'Bearer.{JWT}', and replacing {JWT} with the token, can 'smuggle' it in.
Get a list of all unit IDs in the system.

Not all models have a detailed log. Those that don't will return the same log as the standard version. For models that do have detailed logs, they are usually an archive of various log files and such from the unit. The content type of the response will reflect the format of the archive or text.

delete.description: Most of the models that support detailed logs can not clear them since they are internal logs and message buffers.

get.summary: Get audio RX status and settings.

post.summary: Perform actions on audio RX component.

get.summary: Get all biquads for audio channel.

404.description: Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad
Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad
Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad

/api/v1/moip/audio_rx/{id}/main_bq/{bq}.description: This is an activity optimization endpoint. Setting a biquad here will set the same biquad on every non-sub channel on the audio_rx at the given biquad index. Getting the biquad returns the first non-default biquad on a non-sub channel.

get.summary: Get biquad for main audio channels.

404.description: Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad

put.summary: Update biquad for main audio channels.

404.description: Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad

/api/v1/moip/audio_rx/{id}/sub_bq/{bq}.description: This is an activity optimization endpoint. Setting a biquad here will set the same biquad on every sub channel on the audio_rx at the given biquad index. Getting the biquad returns the first non-default biquad on a sub channel.

get.summary: Get biquad for sub audio channels.

404.description: Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad

put.summary: Update biquad for sub audio channels.

404.description: Unknown AudioRx or channel or biquad

Get video RX status and settings.

get.summary: Get video TX status and settings.
Get IR RX status and settings.

get.summary: Get IR TX status and settings.
Get USB RX status and settings.
Get USB TX status and settings.
Get serial RX status and settings.

get.description: Fetch the next queued serial message received on the endpoint. Does not remove message from queue; issue a delete to pop the next serial message.

delete.description: Pop the next serial message. Ok to do on an empty queue.

get.summary: Get serial TX status and settings.

get.description: Fetch the next queued serial message received on the endpoint. Does not remove message from queue; issue a delete to pop next serial message.

delete.description: Pop the next serial message. Ok to do on an empty queue.

get.summary: Get group RX status and settings.
Get group TX status and settings.

get.description: Get a list of all video wall IDs in the system. Or if search parameters are given, possibly a single vidwall.

get.summary: Get video wall status and settings.

get.description: Get all of the window locations and sizes for a given multiview fixed layout.

AltJwtAuth.description: Alternate location for the JWT for browsers that don't allow modifying the authorization header.
AltJwtAuth.name: x-auth-webtoken

JwtUrlAuth.description: Alternate location for the JWT for browsers that don't allow modifying any headers.
JwtUrlAuth.name: webtoken

message.example: Failed to get data. Error [DEVICE BAD STATE]

name.description: A-z, 0-9, -, and ., but can not start with - or .

tokenType.description: The token use type. Currently only 'Bearer'.

expiresIn.description: Number of seconds until token expires.

DiagType.description: Run a diagnostic mode on the system. 'identify' displays the RX info and TX pairing info on each video RX. 'cycle' does the same information display as 'identify' but also cycles pairing of all RXs to TXs every 5 seconds.

kind.description: The general kind of system from a endpoint standpoint. A system only containing audio endpoints will show as a v1v2.

legacy_api.description: Controls whether the legacy TCP control API is enabled or not.

UpdateFailure.description: Resource update failure. Field may refer to the first field of the update that has an issue.

action.description:
Actions supported:
- reboot_all_units : Reboots every connected unit.

percent.description: Percentage of the upgrade complete. This is a best guess and most likely not very linear.

stage.description: Current state in the upgrade process. Not all units will cycle through every state.

cpu_usage.description: The current cpu usage percentage, or -1 if not supported or currently known.

mem_usage.description: The current memory usage percentage, or -1 if not supported or currently known.

failed_connections.description: The number of failed connections to the unit.

reboots.description: The number of reboots issued to the unit.

temp.description: The unit temperatures in degrees C. The array length can be zero if not supported or currently not known. The number of entries, and what they go to, vary per model.

version.description: Current unit firmware version. Will be empty if unit hasn't been seen since last reboot.

ip.description: Current local IP of unit. Will be empty if unit hasn't been seen since last reboot.

SystemStatus.description: A summary of the system status.

address.description: An optional target address to connect to using the given port. If null or not present then use the same address as was used to reach this interface.

port.description: Port on this device to connect to.

ZoneConfigList.description: Setup of unit zones. Unsupported zone configs are ignored.

bridge_aligned.description: Bridged zone types must be placed on odd start positions (if numbering starts at 1).

zone_count.description: The maximum number of zones that can be configured on the zone config.

channel_count.description: The maximum number of channels that can be configured with the zone config.

version.description: Current unit firmware version. Will be empty if unit hasn't been seen since last reboot.

ip.description: Current local IP of unit. Will be empty if unit hasn't been seen since last reboot.

power_source.description: Current power source for the unit.

supported_led_brightness.description: Does the unit support changing LED brightness. Even on models that do support it, they may not support gradual dimming; the level will be thresholded in that case.

led_brightness.description: Brightness of front panel LEDs if supported. 0 is full off, 1.0 is full brightness.

supported_serial_on_tx.description: Some TR units allow associating the serial interface with either the Rx side or the Tx side.

serial_on_tx.description: Should the serial interface belong to the Rx side or the Tx side. As the name implies, a true value associates it with the Tx side.

supported_ir_on_tx.description: Some TR units allow associating the IR interface with either the Rx side or the Tx side.

ir_on_tx.description: Should the IR interface belong to the Rx side or the Tx side. As the name implies, a true value associates it with the Tx side.

supported_usb_on_tx.description: Some TR units allow associating the USB interface with either the Rx side or the Tx side.

usb_on_tx.description: Should the USB interface belong to the Rx side or the Tx side. As the name implies, a true value associates it with the Tx side.

skip_upgrade.description: Skip upgrading the firmware if the version isn't correct. This is NOT persisted and resets back to false after a reboot. Running mismatched firmware on endpoints is not recommended so this should only be used internally for testing.

disabled.description: Disables connecting to the unit, mainly for testing and debugging. This is NOT persisted and resets back to false after a reboot.

action.description:
Actions supported:
- reboot : Reboot the unit
- clear_log : Clear the unit log
- factory_reset : Factory reset the unit if it supports the operation

UnitAction.description: An action effecting a specific unit.

AudioFormat.description: Not all units can detect which specific type is being sent and may only indicate generic bitstream with the bts option.

range.description: Audio delay range in milliseconds. Values inclusive.
Audio volume offset range in dB. Values inclusive.

default.description: Default audio volume offset in dB.
Default audio crossover slope in dB/octive.

range.description: Audio frequency in hertz. Values inclusive.

AudioLoudnessSupport.description: List of supported audio loudness options.

AudioDownMixSupport.description: List of supported audio downmixing options.

AudioMuteList.description: Output ports that should be muted. Unsupported ports are ignored.

rate.description: Current playback rate. May be 0 if not known or currently playing.

depth.description: Current playback bit depth. May be 0 if not known or currently playing.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

supported.description: Is the generation of a test signal supported.

volume.description: Volume of test signal. Follows limits defined by 'supported_volume'.

volume_offset.description: Sub volume offset in dBs of amplification.

supported_biquad_count.description: Number of adjustable biquads per channel.

action.description:
Actions supported:
- volume_up : Increase the volume level by one.
- volume_down : Decrease the volume level by one.

AudioRxAction.description: An action effecting a specific audio RX endpoint.

AudioChannelKind.description: Audio channel description. The 'r_*' are the rear channels. 'unknown' can be returned if more channels then are valid are set.

AudioChannelType.description: Audio channel description. The 'speaker' is a regular driven channel. 'bpos' is bridged positive, and 'bneg' is bridged negative. 'line' is a line level 'rca' output. 'unknown' can be returned if more channels then are valid are set.

volume_offset.description: Channel volume offset in dBs of amplification.

test_enabled.description: Per channel test tone. Only supported if test tone is supported on the RX (see the /settings/test/supported field). The RX test volume setting also applies to the per channel tone (see the /settings/test/volume field).

freq.description: Operating frequency in hertz. Use varies based on filter type.

q.description: Filter Q value. Use varies based on filter type.

gain.description: Filter gain value. Use varies based on filter type.

AudioSourcePriorityList.description: Selected source port with priority fallback order. Unsupported ports are ignored. Not all units support fallback.

range.description: Audio gain range in dB. Values inclusive.
Audio level range in volts. Values inclusive.

AudioEDIDSupport.description: List of supported audio EDID options.

rate.description: Current recording rate. Maybe be different than the specified rate if the input is toslink, for example.

depth.description: Current recording bit depth. Maybe be different than the specified depth if the input is toslink, for example.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

enabled.description: Is the output enabled. This disables most functionallity but its real use is for hiding the RX on the UI if disabled and never wanted. This is used for the audio return, for example.

level.description: Output level in volts. This would only apply to pre-amp outputs.

gain.description: Audio input gain in dB. Added after any supported input volume level. May be used at volume levels not at 100, but intended usage is amplification above 100.

level.description: Signal sense level on analog source in volts.

edid.description: The maximum format advertised in the EDID audio block. This setting only applies to the HDMI source if its supported.

VideoWallModeType.description: Format of the video wall params field.

VideoWallModeSupport.description: List of supported video wall modes.

SerialFormatType.description: How the message is encoded. packed has no separators. raw is as is; this may have issues with non-ascii values.

PipPositionType.description: General position of PiP window on screen.

PipEncodingType.description: Controls how the PiP stream is decoded. 'auto' will autodetect the stream encoding and decode accordingly, but this takes startup time. Both 'h264' and 'h265' only decode that type, which is quicker to start, but must match the stream encoding.

range.description: PiP window width as a percentage. Values inclusive.

default.description: Default PiP window width as a percentage.

condition.description: Condition message for stream status, or empty if nothing to report. This currently only states the reason a stream isn't working, but this could expand.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

pip_paired_tx.description: This is the VideoTx to pair a possible PiP window to. Only has an effect if supported.

pip_paired_url.description: This is the h265/h265 URL to pair a possible PiP window to. This has precendence over regular PiP pairing.

mv_paired_tx.description: These are the VideoTx to pair each window in a multiview to.

osd.description: Text message to show onscreen. Set to null to show nothing.

enabled.description: Enable application of the video transformation. Allows disabling of settings without clearing them.

params.description: For mode v1, the values are (M-1, N-1, X, Y) where M is the number of displays across, N is the number of displays vertically, X is the display column index (0 to M-1 inclusive), and Y is the display row index (0 to N-1 inclusive). For mode v2, the values are (X_TL, Y_TL, X_BR, Y_BR) where X_TL is the top-left X coordinate, Y_TL is the top-left Y coordinate, X_BR is the bottom-left X coordinate, and Y_BR is the bottom-right Y coordinate. The coordinates are on a virtual canvas of 0-10000 by 0-10000.

moninfo.description: Monitor bezel adjustments. Values are sizes in millimeters in the order: inner width, outer width, inner height, outer height.

method.description: What to do, if anything, on the loss of the video stream. Any effect takes place after the loss of the stream and the timeout period expires. serial sends the serial_on/serial_off message out of the associated serial port. cec sends the tv_on/tv_off cec commands out of the hdmi port.

output.description: The state of the hdmi output signal after the timeout expires.

timeout.description: Loss of signal timeout in seconds.

supported_url.description: Is a URL PiP window supported.

width.description: The PiP window width as a percentage.

format.description: Format of the CEC transmission to send. hex_colon is hex encoded byte values separated with colons. hex_space is hex encoded byte values separated with spaces. tv_on and tv_off are canned transmissions and must have a null message value.

VideoEDIDSupport.description: List of supported video EDID options.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

supported_preferred.description: Does this unit support setting a preferred RX to source an EDID from.

preferred.description: A VideoRx to source a display EDID from to be presented to the video source. Tihs takes precedence, so 'video_format' is only used when this is unpaired.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

static_routing.description: This setting controls whether a group pairing will apply to the Ir rx. This enables the existing feature of having a static pairing or follow the video rx.

format.description: Format of the IR transmission to send.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.
Parent unit this component belongs to.

static_routing.description: This setting controls whether a group pairing will apply to the Usb rx. This enables the existing feature of having a static pairing or follow the video rx.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.
Parent unit this component belongs to.

paired_capture.description: Serial responses are captured and sent out as controller messages. This overrides endpoint pairing. This should also be set if serial message are to be sent with the API; not all endpoint types allow serial to be programatically sent without also being in capture mode.

messages.description: The number of serial messages received on endpoint queued. This queue is limited so messages should be read out of the /message url in a timely fashion. Every message received will generate a state change on this url even if the message count has hit its limit. A common approach to handling the messages is to either wait for a state change on this url, or poll it at an interval, and if the count isn't 0, then do a GET/DELETE/... cycle on the /message url until it returns a 204.

static_routing.description: This setting controls whether a group pairing will apply to the Serial rx. This enables the existing feature of having a static pairing or follow the Video rx.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

paired_capture.description: Serial responses are captured and sent out as controller messages. This overrides endpoint pairing. This should also be set if serial message are to be sent with the API; not all endpoint types allow serial to be programatically sent without also being in capture mode.

messages.description: The number of serial messages received on endpoint queued. This queue is limited so messages should be read out of the /message url in a timely fashion. Every message received will generate a state change on this url even if the message count has hit its limit. A common approach to handling the messages is to either wait for a state change on this url, or poll it at an interval, and if the count isn't 0, then do a GET/DELETE/... cycle on the /message url until it returns a 204.

GroupRx.description: A group of associated end points. This is usually the same as the endpoints on a unit for single zone units, but this is not the case for multizone units. This is only a hint and does not require these to be used together.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

vidwall.description: If, and which, video wall this group is a member of. As the read only state indicates, this back reference is managed automatically.

paired_tx.description: This is the GroupTx to pair to. All endpoints in this group will be paired to the endpoints in the target group of like kind. If the individual endpoints are paired directly, this entry may no longer be accurate. A null value is an unpaired state.

pip_paired_tx.description: This is the GroupTx to pair a possible PiP window to. The audio endpoint pairing is controlled by the 'audio_selected' fields below. The video endpoint pairing is always done, but may not have any effect if PiP isn't supported; check the video_rx for support. If the individual endpoints are paired directly, this entry may no longer be accurate. A null value is an unpaired state.

pip_paired_url.description: This is the h265/h265 URL to pair a possible PiP window to. This has precendence over endpoint pairing. The video endpoint pairing is always done, but may not have any effect if PiP isn't supported; check the video_rx for support. If the individual endpoints are paired directly, this entry may no longer be accurate. A null value or an empty string is an unpaired state.

mv_paired_tx.description: These are the GroupTx to pair each window in a multiview to. There should be as many entries as there are windows in the current multiview layout. Extra entries are ignored and missing entries are treated as unpaired windows. A null value is an unpaired state.

index.description: The RX matrix index number. Only used for mapping to control systems that require index positions.

enabled.description: Enable/disable PiP window. Enable pairs the video_rx to the PiP pairing and enables it, if supported. Audio switches to the PiP pairing depending on the 'audio_selected' field. Disable returns both to the standard 'paired_tx' pairings.

audio_selected.description: Controls whether the audio is paired from the PiP pairing or from the standard 'paired_tx' pairing. This is gated by the 'enabled' field.

enabled.description: Enable/disable multiview mode. Enable pairs the video_rx to the multiview pairings and enables it, if supported. Audio switches to the window selected by the 'audio_selected' field. Disable returns both to the standard 'paired_tx' pairings.

audio_selected.description: Controls which window is used for audio pairing. This is gated by the 'enabled' field.

state.description: This is a synthesized state representing all of the RXs.

GroupTx.description: A group of associated end points. This is usually the same as the endpoints on a unit for single zone units, but this is not the case for multizone units. This is only a hint and does not require these to be used together.

unit.description: Parent unit this component belongs to.

index.description: The TX matrix index number. Only used for mapping to control systems that require index positions.

state.description: This is a synthesized state representing all of the TXs.

group_rx.description: The assigned GroupRxs for the video wall. The groups don't have to have a VideoRx association but the entry will still take up the location in the video wall. There should be width x height rxs given. Any group can only be in one video wall at a time and update will fail if given an RX already in a video wall. On initial add the RXs will have their video wall settings set to v1 mode with placement values for row-column order. The settings won't be changed after this point so they may be refined on the RXs directly.

paired_tx.description: This is the GroupTx to pair to. When set, each of the RXs will be paired to this source and their video wall settings will be enabled if not already. This can allow individual RXs in the video wall to be switched away, and most likely have their video wall settings disabled, from the video wall role, but a new pairing will put it back.

state.description: This is a synthesized state representing all of the RXs.

index.description: The RX matrix index number. Only used for mapping to control systems that require index positions.

height.description: Number of displays top to bottom.

CreateFailure.description: Resource create failure. Field may refer to the first field of the create that has an issue.

name.description: A semi-descriptive name for the layout.

EdidResponse.description: Current EDID of the output sink. This may be empty if the RX doesn't support fetching the EDID or if there is no attached sink. The EDID is also checked for checksum errors.

VideoWallListResponse.description: List of VideoWalls or a single VideoWall if search parameters used.

SupportedParam.name: supported
SupportedParam.description: A zero value removes 'supported' fields, any other value, or no parameter, leaves them in.

DetailedParam.name: detailed
DetailedParam.description: A non-zero value specifies the detailed version, if possible. Otherwise the standard version is targeted.

Channel.name: channel
Channel.description: Audio channel index. Numbered from 0 to 'supported_channel_count'-1 on the audio_rx.

BiQuad.name: bq
BiQuad.description: Audio channel biquad index. Numbered from 0 to 'supported_biquad_count'-1 on the audio_rx.

IndexParam.name: index
IndexParam.description: Lookup an entry by its index position value.
